# Reliable Indicator Approach

**Node Type:** synthesis
**Node ID:** d3b0eb8b-eff3-4944-8479-8233c995fb80
**Depth:** 5
**Terminal:** No

**Parent:** [Can a true belief arrived at by unreliable virtue be considered knowledge?](can-a-true-belief-arrived-at-by-unreliable-virtue-be-considered-knowledge-antithesis-77622b68-dde0-4f16-8d6f-e09af6b88c84.md)

## Content

**Knowledge is true belief generated by an intellectual virtue that acts as a reliable indicator of truth**, **For a person to know a proposition, it is necessary that they believe the proposition**, **For a person to know a proposition, it is necessary for the proposition to be true**, **An intellectual virtue should be a reliable indicator of the truth across a range of scenarios**, **For a person to know a proposition, it is sufficient that they believe it, the proposition is true, and their intellectual virtue serves as a reliable indicator of truth across scenarios**
